_description: Proven UGC hook frameworks for ad concepts. Used by the concept generator to create ad ideas matched to brand + product + audience.

shocked_discovery.name: Shocked Discovery
shocked_discovery.description: Character discovers something unexpected about the product's effect. The 'wait, is this actually working?' moment
text_overlay_examples: POV: day 14 of this shake
when the supplement actually works
me realizing I haven't felt this good in years
shocked_discovery.performance_notes: Strong hook rate. The frozen moment creates curiosity gap

skeptic_to_believer.name: Skeptic to Believer
skeptic_to_believer.description: Character starts doubtful, ends convinced. The conversion arc
skeptic_to_believer.emotional_arc: Eye roll / doubt → trying it → surprise → genuine recommendation
text_overlay_examples: I thought this was just another overpriced supplement
my friend wouldn't stop talking about this so I tried it
ok I owe everyone an apology
frame_sequence: Skeptical face (arms crossed or eye roll)

secret_share.name: Secret Share
secret_share.description: Character leans in to share something they don't want everyone to know about. Conspiratorial, exclusive energy
secret_share.emotional_arc: Checking surroundings → leaning in → whispering/sharing → 'trust me' nod
best_for: Products that feel like insider knowledge
Niche products not everyone knows about
text_overlay_examples: don't let this go viral but...
the supplement my nutritionist actually takes
ok I'm gatekeeping this but you need to know

mind_blown.name: Mind Blown
mind_blown.description: Character just learned something that changes how they think about the product/category. Information revelation
mind_blown.emotional_arc: Reading/watching something → eyes widen → processing → 'oh my god' realization
text_overlay_examples: when you find out what's actually in AG1 vs this
POV: you just calculated the cost per ingredient
me reading the actual research on this

morning_routine.name: Morning Routine
morning_routine.description: Character shows their morning routine featuring the product. Aspirational but achievable. 'Day in my life' energy
morning_routine.emotional_arc: Waking up → routine steps → product moment → feeling good / ready for day
best_for: Anything that fits into a routine
text_overlay_examples: my non-negotiable morning routine
5am morning routine that changed my energy
the one thing I never skip
frame_sequence: Feeling good / leaving for day / CTA

problem_agitate_solve.name: Problem → Agitate → Solve
problem_agitate_solve.description: Start with the pain point, make it worse, then present the product as the solution
problem_agitate_solve.emotional_arc: Frustrated → more frustrated → discovery → relief / satisfaction
best_for: Products that solve clear pain points
text_overlay_examples: I was spending $150/month on 5 different supplements
my cabinet looked like a pharmacy
then I found the one that replaces them all
frame_sequence: Showing the problem (too many bottles, complicated routine)

before_after_subtle.name: Before/After (Subtle)
before_after_subtle.description: Two frames showing subtle, believable change. NOT dramatic transformation. Real results energy
before_after_subtle.emotional_arc: Tired/dull (before) → healthier/brighter (after). The difference is REAL not magical
text_overlay_examples: 2 weeks apart
same lighting, same camera, different glow
the subtle difference no one talks about
before_after_subtle.performance_notes: CRITICAL: subtle differences only. Dramatic = fake = scroll

unboxing_first_impression.name: Unboxing / First Impression
unboxing_first_impression.description: Opening the product for the first time. Genuine first reaction to packaging, smell, taste
unboxing_first_impression.emotional_arc: Curious → opening → first impression → surprised by quality
text_overlay_examples: finally trying the shake everyone's been talking about
first impression of LYFEfuel
is this worth $2 a serving? let's find out
